channel.title: Follow Builders — X/Twitter
channel.description: AI builders' latest tweets, curated by follow-builders

item.title: Peter Yang (@petergyang): ChatGPT Images works great from the mobile app, but when I try to generate images on @ChatGPTapp web...
item.description:
ChatGPT Images works great from the mobile app, but when I try to generate images on @ChatGPTapp web - it often forgets it has access to the image tool and start generating code instead, resulting in "images" like this lol
Seems like a bug please fix. https://t.co/0a5STt0aCV
Bio: Practical AI tutorials and interviews for busy people | Join 140K+ readers at https://t.co/XYKTmGVH14 | Product at Roblox ❤️ 22 🔁 1

item.title:
Amjad Masad (@amasad): Update on my lawsuit against Congressman Randy Fine.
He tried to weasel out of the case by unblocki...
item.description:
Update on my lawsuit against Congressman Randy Fine.
He tried to weasel out of the case by unblocking me, but this isn’t just about me. It’s about Fine’s DISRESPECT for the 1st Amendment.
We will not stop until he’s forced to unblock all Americans and respect their rights. https://t.co/sTPhkbk8kH https://t.co/kYaTv5K656
Bio: ceo @replit. civilizationist ❤️ 1352 🔁 132

item.title: Aaron Levie (@levie): If you read this and don’t understand why it’s happening it’s an opportunity to reset your understan...
item.description:
If you read this and don’t understand why it’s happening it’s an opportunity to reset your understanding of how the real world works.
The real world will need a ton of help actually getting agents going in the enterprise. Companies have legacy tech stacks they need to modernize, data in tons of fragmented tools, knowledge that isn’t captured or digitized, and change management needed to actually utilize agents effectively. And they have to do all this while still running their business day-to-day, unlike startups.
This is why there is so much opportunity for companies (software or services) to actually deploy agents in specific domains and workflows. This remains a big opportunity for both existing services providers but also tons of new startups as well. Every new technology wave produces a new era of consulting firms that can deliver on that technology.
It’s also why the FDE model is going to be alive and well for a long time because companies will want to have their vendor actually help drive the change management and implementation for their new workflows.
The people aren’t going away. Far from it.
Bio: ceo @box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI ❤️ 1173 🔁 102

item.title: Zara Zhang (@zarazhangrui): Several old books that are surprisingly relevant to what's happening in AI today: 1. The Mythical Ma...
item.description:
Several old books that are surprisingly relevant to what's happening in AI today: 1. The Mythical Man-Month (1975 book by Fred Brooks) 2. Diffusion of Innovations (1962 book by Everett M Rogerss) 3. Player Piano (1952 book by Kurt Vonnegut)
Bio: Builder. Dangerously skips permissions. Harvard’17. GitHub: https://t.co/KCuEajezlL YouTube: https://t.co/8xzbGWtf6w ❤️ 9 🔁 1

Bio: Builder. Dangerously skips permissions. Harvard’17. GitHub: https://t.co/KCuEajezlL YouTube: https://t.co/8xzbGWtf6w ❤️ 204 🔁 13

item.title: Zara Zhang (@zarazhangrui): Have you ever felt like you have more agents/AI tools than you have work for them to do
item.description:

item.title: Nikunj Kothari (@nikunj): Double opt in is cool, but I highly highly recommend giving 10-15 people you trust FULL blind intro...
item.description:
Double opt in is cool, but I highly highly recommend giving 10-15 people you trust FULL blind intro privilege.
It’s the fastest way to maximize serendipity.
It’s the single best thing I’ve done to meet some of the most fascinating people!
Bio: partner @fpvventures - investing in seed/A. previous: early hire @meter, @opendoor, @atlassian & others. love @shimoleejhaveri + 👦👧 ❤️ 56 🔁 2

item.title:
Peter Steinberger (@steipete): OpenClaw 2026.4.21 is live.
Small release, important fix: npm updates now repair bundled plugin run...
item.description:
OpenClaw 2026.4.21 is live.
Small release, important fix: npm updates now repair bundled plugin runtime deps, with Docker E2E coverage so Telegram/Discord/Slack do not break after upgrade.
Also backports OpenAI Image 2 support.
npm i -g openclaw@latest
Bio: Polyagentmorous ClawFather. Came back from retirement to mess with AI and help a lobster take over the world. @OpenClaw🦞 + @OpenAI ❤️ 492 🔁 17

item.title: Peter Steinberger (@steipete): Did some work to get our CI times down from 8 to two minutes via some... parallelization. Kudos to t...
item.description:
Did some work to get our CI times down from 8 to two minutes via some... parallelization. Kudos to the @useblacksmith folks for sponsoring + letting us melt their servers. https://t.co/CkrAuTDsK1
Bio: Polyagentmorous ClawFather. Came back from retirement to mess with AI and help a lobster take over the world. @OpenClaw🦞 + @OpenAI ❤️ 243 🔁 8

item.title:
Peter Steinberger (@steipete): discrawl 0.3.0 is out.
This release adds Git-backed archive sync, so a Discord archive can be publi...
item.description:
discrawl 0.3.0 is out.
This release adds Git-backed archive sync, so a Discord archive can be published to a private repo and queried locally without every user needing bot credentials.
Also: auto-refresh, activity reports, field notes, faster imports. https://t.co/ZFGgZEFtFQ
Bio: Polyagentmorous ClawFather. Came back from retirement to mess with AI and help a lobster take over the world. @OpenClaw🦞 + @OpenAI ❤️ 147 🔁 8
